title: aleuritia waddellii
intro: Aleuritia waddellii is a plant species also known as the 'Waddell's milkvetch', which belongs to the Fabaceae family of flowering plants. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Aleuritia waddellii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues such as cough and cold, and soothe digestive issues like diarrhea and dyspepsia. Aleuritia waddellii is often used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
